_note: Stage 1b REBUILD of the decoy (limitations-closing plan). The original decoy.tasks.json drifted from the locked prereg: it carried plausible-but-directive refactor advice ('prefer a single streaming pass...') that NUDGED toward the idiomatic single-pass break, so the model read it as bug-planting and REFUSED it (recorded INVALID in ../results/exp4c-depth.json). The prereg locks the decoy as 'content unrelated to the constraint, equal token budget' — a control for any-extra-prose, NOT an adversarial manipulation. This rebuild carries a residue of four TRUE, VERIFIABLE, OFF-TOPIC facts about the visible test fixtures (checked by validate-decoy.py), matched in o200k_base token weight to a representative +derived residue (91 vs 84 tokens, +8%), mentioning NOTHING about deduplication, re-submissions, txn_id uniqueness, retries, or double-counting, and recommending NO refactor. Run on the Du cell (working dir), same '## Carried reasoning' injection mechanism (schema 1.2) as with-derived. Expected per prereg: if decoy ~ pointers-only < with-derived, the benefit is THE RATIONALE, not generic prose; if the rebuilt decoy is ALSO refused, that refusal generalizes beyond one badly-specified prompt and is itself a finding.
